channel.title: Capstone on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Capstone on RaihnForge

item.title: From Intuition to Intention: My Journey in Project Management
item.description:
Prepared for ADMG 479, Project Management Maturity, for Professor Dr. Laura Williamson. Drafted by Joshua Keyes, June 4, 2025.

Introduction

My journey through the Project Management specialization at Central Washington University has been one of steady growth, practical application, and transformative learning. With a diverse professional background spanning design, compliance, and team leadership, I entered this program with a desire to formalize my management experience and refine my skills in organizational planning, execution, and strategic communication. This reflection paper serves as a capstone for that passage, offering a comprehensive reflection on how this specialization shaped my growth as a project manager.

item.title: Sustain Craft: A Mobile-Game Pitch for Sustainability Literacy
item.description:
A note on form. This artifact is a pitch deck, not a prose essay. What follows describes each section of the deck in the order it was presented, preserving the speaker notes and the verbatim character bio for the protagonist.

1. Pitch

Title. Sustain Craft, a mobile game pitch.

Capstone framing. Application of skills for positive change.

Why this medium. The deck opens by tracing the skills the game draws on: Marine Corps leader, graphic designer and illustrator, animator, game designer, project leader. The argument is that games are the natural medium for me to make a civic argument with, because I already have most of the disciplinary skills the medium demands.
